name: phonlp

metadata.name: phonlp
metadata.summary: PhoNLP: A joint multi-task learning model for Vietnamese part-of-speech tagging, named entity recognition and dependency parsing

documentation:
- [Introduction](#introduction) - [Usage example: Command lines](#command) - [Usage example: Python API](#api) - [Pre-trained PhoNLP model](#pretrained)

# PhoNLP: A BERT-based multi-task learning model for part-of-speech tagging, named entity recognition and dependency parsing PhoNLP is a multi-task learning model for joint part-of-speech (POS) tagging, named entity recognition (NER) and dependency parsing. Experiments on Vietnamese benchmark datasets show that PhoNLP produces state-of-the-art results, outperforming a single-task learning approach that fine-tunes the pre-trained Vietnamese language model [PhoBERT](https://github.com/VinAIResearch/PhoBERT) for each task independently. _Although we evaluate PhoNLP on Vietnamese, our usage examples below can directly work for other languages that have gold annotated corpora available for the three tasks of POS tagging, NER and dependency parsing, and a pre-trained BERT-based language model available from [transformers](https://huggingface.co/models) (e.g. BERT, mBERT, RoBERTa, XLM-RoBERTa)._

Details of the PhoNLP model architecture and experimental results can be found in our [following paper](https://aclanthology.org/2021.naacl-demos.1/): @inproceedings{phonlp, title = {{PhoNLP: A joint multi-task learning model for Vietnamese part-of-speech tagging, named entity recognition and dependency parsing}}, author = {Linh The Nguyen and Dat Quoc Nguyen}, booktitle = {Proceedings of the 2021 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Demonstrations}, pages = {1--7}, year = {2021} } **Please CITE** our paper when PhoNLP is used to help produce published results or incorporated into other software. ## Installation - Python version >= 3.6; PyTorch version >= 1.4.0 - PhoNLP can be installed using `pip` as follows: `pip3 install phonlp` - Or PhoNLP can also be installed from source with the following commands: ``` git clone https://github.com/VinAIResearch/PhoNLP cd PhoNLP pip3 install -e . ``` ## Usage example: Command lines To play with the examples using command lines, please install `phonlp` from the source: ``` git clone https://github.com/VinAIResearch/PhoNLP cd PhoNLP pip3 install -e . ``` ### Training ``` cd phonlp/models python3 run_phonlp.py --mode train --save_dir \ --pretrained_lm \ --lr --batch_size --num_epoch \ --lambda_pos --lambda_ner --lambda_dep \ --train_file_pos --eval_file_pos \ --train_file_ner --eval_file_ner \ --train_file_dep --eval_file_dep ``` `--lambda_pos`, `--lambda_ner` and `--lambda_dep` represent mixture weights associated with POS tagging, NER and dependency parsing losses, respectively, and `lambda_pos + lambda_ner + lambda_dep = 1`. Example: ``` cd phonlp/models python3 run_phonlp.py --mode train --save_dir ./phonlp_tmp \ --pretrained_lm "vinai/phobert-base" \ --lr 1e-5 --batch_size 32 --num_epoch 40 \ --lambda_pos 0.4 --lambda_ner 0.2 --lambda_dep 0.4 \ --train_file_pos ../sample_data/pos_train.txt --eval_file_pos ../sample_data/pos_valid.txt \ --train_file_ner ../sample_data/ner_train.txt --eval_file_ner ../sample_data/ner_valid.txt \ --train_file_dep ../sample_data/dep_train.conll --eval_file_dep ../sample_data/dep_valid.conll ``` ### Evaluation ``` cd phonlp/models python3 run_phonlp.py --mode eval --save_dir \ --batch_size \ --eval_file_pos \ --eval_file_ner \ --eval_file_dep ``` Example: ``` cd phonlp/models python3 run_phonlp.py --mode eval --save_dir ./phonlp_tmp \ --batch_size 8 \ --eval_file_pos ../sample_data/pos_test.txt \ --eval_file_ner ../sample_data/ner_test.txt \ --eval_file_dep ../sample_data/dep_test.conll ``` ### Annotate a corpus ``` cd phonlp/models python3 run_phonlp.py --mode annotate --save_dir \ --batch_size \ --input_file \ --output_file ``` Example: ``` cd phonlp/models python3 run_phonlp.py --mode annotate --save_dir ./phonlp_tmp \ --batch_size 8 \ --input_file ../sample_data/input.txt \ --output_file ../sample_data/output.txt ``` ## Usage example: Python API ```python import phonlp # Load the trained PhoNLP model model = phonlp.load(save_dir='/absolute/path/to/phonlp_tmp') # Annotate a corpus where each line represents a word-segmented sentence model.annotate(input_file='/absolute/path/to/input.txt', output_file='/absolute/path/to/output.txt') # Annotate a word-segmented sentence model.print_out(model.annotate(text="Tôi đang làm_việc tại VinAI .")) ``` By default, the output for each input sentence is formatted with 6 columns representing word index, word form, POS tag, NER label, head index of the current word and its dependency relation type: ``` 1 Tôi P O 3 sub 2 đang R O 3 adv 3 làm_việc V O 0 root 4 tại E O 3 loc 5 VinAI Np B-ORG 4 prob 6 . CH O 3 punct ``` The output can be formatted following the 10-column CoNLL format where the last column is used to represent NER predictions. This can be done by adding `output_type='conll'` into the `model.annotate()` function. Also, in the `model.annotate()` function, the value of the parameter `batch_size` can be adjusted to fit your computer's memory instead of using the default one at 1 (`batch_size=1`). Here, a larger `batch_size` would lead to a faster performance speed. ## Pre-trained PhoNLP model for Vietnamese - The pre-trained PhoNLP model for Vietnamese can be manually downloaded from [HERE](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wfVGVsKkg7sH8R_3ECRoQ46DVOAmsNkQ/view?usp=sharing). - Or it can be downloaded as follows: ```python import phonlp # Automatically download the pre-trained PhoNLP model for Vietnamese # and save it in a local machine folder phonlp.download(save_dir='/absolute/path/to/pretrained_phonlp') # Load the pre-trained PhoNLP model for Vietnamese model = phonlp.load(save_dir='/absolute/path/to/pretrained_phonlp') # Annotate a corpus where each line represents a word-segmented sentence model.annotate(input_file='/absolute/path/to/input.txt', output_file='/absolute/path/to/output.txt') # Annotate a word-segmented sentence model.print_out(model.annotate(text="Tôi đang làm_việc tại VinAI .")) ``` ### Using VnCoreNLP to perform word and sentence segmentation on raw Vietnamese texts In case the input Vietnamese texts are `raw`, i.e. without word and sentence segmentation, a word segmenter must be applied to produce word-segmented sentences before feeding to the pre-trained PhoNLP model for Vietnamese. Users should use [VnCoreNLP](https://github.com/vncorenlp/VnCoreNLP) to perform word and sentence segmentation (as it produces the same Vietnamese tone normalization that was applied to the data of the POS tagging, NER and dependency parsing tasks). #### Installation pip3 install py_vncorenlp #### Example usage ```python import py_vncorenlp # Automatically download VnCoreNLP components from the original repository # and save them in some local machine folder py_vncorenlp.download_model(save_dir='/absolute/path/to/vncorenlp') # Load VnCoreNLP for word and sentence segmentation rdrsegmenter = py_vncorenlp.VnCoreNLP(annotators=["wseg"], save_dir='/absolute/path/to/vncorenlp') # Perform word and sentence segmentation print(rdrsegmenter.word_segment("Ông Nguyễn Khắc Chúc đang làm việc tại Đại học Quốc gia Hà Nội. Bà Lan, vợ ông Chúc, cũng làm việc tại đây.")) # ['Ông Nguyễn_Khắc_Chúc đang làm_việc tại Đại_học Quốc_gia Hà_Nội .', 'Bà Lan , vợ ông Chúc , cũng làm_việc tại đây .'] ```

modules.name: phonlp.models.common.crf
documentation: CRF loss and viterbi decoding.

functions.name: phonlp.models.common.crf.viterbi_decode
documentation:
Decode a tag sequence with viterbi algorithm.
scores: seq_len x num_tags (numpy array)
transition_params: num_tags x num_tags (numpy array)
@return:
    viterbi: a list of tag ids with highest score
    viterbi_score: the highest score

functions.name: phonlp.models.common.crf.log_sum_exp
documentation:
Numerically stable implementation of the operation
value.exp().sum(dim, keepdim).log()

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.crf.CRFLoss

documentation:
inputs: batch_size x seq_len x num_tags
masks: batch_size x seq_len
tag_indices: batch_size x seq_len

@return:
    loss: CRF negative log likelihood on all instances.
    transitions: the transition matrix

documentation:
@return:

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.crf.CRFLoss.crf_binary_score
documentation:
@return:
    binary_scores: batch_size

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.crf.CRFLoss.crf_log_norm
documentation:
Calculate the CRF partition in log space for each instance, following:
    http://www.cs.columbia.edu/~mcollins/fb.pdf
@return:
    log_norm: batch_size

Calculate log-space crf loss, given unary potentials, a transition matrix
and gold tag sequences.

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.dropout.WordDropout
documentation:
A word dropout layer that's designed for embedded inputs (e.g., any inputs to an LSTM layer).
Given a batch of embedded inputs, this layer randomly set some of them to be a replacement state.
Note that this layer assumes the last dimension of the input to be the hidden dimension of a unit.

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.dropout.LockedDropout
documentation:
A variant of dropout layer that consistently drops out the same parameters over time. Also known as the variational dropout.
This implentation was modified from the LockedDropout implementation in the flair library (https://github.com/zalandoresearch/flair).

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.dropout.SequenceUnitDropout

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.dropout.SequenceUnitDropout.forward
documentation: :param: x must be a LongTensor of unit indices.

A unit dropout layer that's designed for input of sequence units (e.g., word sequence, char sequence, etc.).
Given a sequence of unit indices, this layer randomly set some of them to be a replacement id (usually set to be <UNK>).

modules.name: phonlp.models.common.loss
documentation: Different loss functions.

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.loss.MixLoss
documentation:
A mixture of SequenceLoss and CrossEntropyLoss.
Loss = SequenceLoss + alpha * CELoss

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.loss.MaxEntropySequenceLoss

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.loss.MaxEntropySequenceLoss.forward
documentation:
inputs: [N, C]
targets: [N]

A max entropy loss that encourage the model to have large entropy,
therefore giving more diverse outputs.

Loss = NLLLoss + alpha * EntropyLoss

modules.name: phonlp.models.common.seq2seq_constant
documentation: Constants for seq2seq models.

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.biaffine.PairwiseBilinear
documentation:
A bilinear module that deals with broadcasting for efficient memory usage.
Input: tensors of sizes (N x L1 x D1) and (N x L2 x D2)
Output: tensor of size (N x L1 x L2 x O)

modules.name: phonlp.models.common.chuliu_edmonds

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.BaseVocab

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.BaseVocab.state_dict
documentation:
Returns a dictionary containing all states that are necessary to recover
this vocab. Useful for serialization.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.BaseVocab.load_state_dict
documentation: Returns a new Vocab instance constructed from a state dict.

A base class for common vocabulary operations. Each subclass should at least
implement its own build_vocab() function.

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.CompositeVocab
documentation:
Vocabulary class that handles parsing and printing composite values such as
compositional XPOS and universal morphological features (UFeats).

Two key options are `keyed` and `sep`. `sep` specifies the separator used between
different parts of the composite values, which is `|` for UFeats, for example.
If `keyed` is `True`, then the incoming value is treated similarly to UFeats, where
each part is a key/value pair separated by an equal sign (`=`). There are no inherit
order to the keys, and we sort them alphabetically for serialization and deserialization.
Whenever a part is absent, its internal value is a special `<EMPTY>` symbol that will
be treated accordingly when generating the output. If `keyed` is `False`, then the parts
are treated as positioned values, and `<EMPTY>` is used to pad parts at the end when the
incoming value is not long enough.

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.BaseMultiVocab

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.BaseMultiVocab.state_dict
documentation: Build a state dict by iteratively calling state_dict() of all vocabs.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.vocab.BaseMultiVocab.load_state_dict
documentation: Construct a MultiVocab by reading from a state dict.

A convenient vocab container that can store multiple BaseVocab instances, and support
safe serialization of all instances via state dicts. Each subclass of this base class
should implement the load_state_dict() function to specify how a saved state dict
should be loaded back.

modules.name: phonlp.models.ner.utils
documentation: Utility functions for dealing with NER tagging.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: phonlp.models.ner.utils.is_bio_scheme
documentation:
Check if BIO tagging scheme is used. Return True if so.

Args:
    all_tags: a list of NER tags

Returns:
    True if the tagging scheme is BIO, otherwise False

functions.name: phonlp.models.ner.utils.to_bio2
documentation:
Convert the original tag sequence to BIO2 format. If the input is already in BIO2 format,
the original input is returned.

Args:
    tags: a list of tags in either BIO or BIO2 format

Returns:
    new_tags: a list of tags in BIO2 format

functions.name: phonlp.models.ner.utils.bio2_to_bioes
documentation:
Convert the BIO2 tag sequence into a BIOES sequence.

Args:
    tags: a list of tags in BIO2 format

Returns:
    new_tags: a list of tags in BIOES format

functions.name: phonlp.models.ner.utils.decode_from_bioes
documentation:
Decode from a sequence of BIOES tags, assuming default tag is 'O'.
Args:
    tags: a list of BIOES tags

Returns:
    A list of dict with start_idx, end_idx, and type values.

modules.name: phonlp.models.ner.scorer
documentation: An NER scorer that calculates F1 score given gold and predicted tags.

functions.name: phonlp.models.ner.scorer.score_by_entity
documentation:
Score predicted tags at the entity level.

Args:
    pred_tags_sequences: a list of list of predicted tags for each word
    gold_tags_sequences: a list of list of gold tags for each word
    verbose: print log with results

Returns:
    Precision, recall and F1 scores.

functions.name: phonlp.models.ner.scorer.score_by_token
documentation:
Score predicted tags at the token level.

Args:
    pred_tags_sequences: a list of list of predicted tags for each word
    gold_tags_sequences: a list of list of gold tags for each word
    verbose: print log with results

Returns:
    Precision, recall and F1 scores.

modules.name: phonlp.models.common.doc
documentation: Basic data structures

classes.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.__init__
documentation:
Construct a document given a list of sentences in the form of lists of CoNLL-U dicts.

Args:
    sentences: a list of sentences, which being a list of token entry, in the form of a CoNLL-U dict.
    text: the raw text of the document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.text
documentation: Access the raw text for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.text
documentation: Set the raw text for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.sentences
documentation: Access the list of sentences for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.sentences
documentation: Set the list of tokens for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.num_tokens
documentation: Access the number of tokens for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.num_tokens
documentation: Set the number of tokens for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.num_words
documentation: Access the number of words for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.num_words
documentation: Set the number of words for this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.ents
documentation: Access the list of entities in this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.ents
documentation: Set the list of entities in this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.entities
documentation: Access the list of entities. This is just an alias of `ents`.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.entities
documentation: Set the list of entities in this document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.get
documentation:
Get fields from a list of field names. If only one field name is provided, return a list
of that field; if more than one, return a list of list. Note that all returned fields are after
multi-word expansion.

Args:
    fields: name of the fields as a list
    as_sentences: if True, return the fields as a list of sentences; otherwise as a whole list
    from_token: if True, get the fields from Token; otherwise from Word

Returns:
    All requested fields.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.set
documentation:
Set fields based on contents. If only one field (singleton list) is provided, then a list
of content will be expected; otherwise a list of list of contents will be expected.

Args:
    fields: name of the fields as a list
    contents: field values to set; total length should be equal to number of words/tokens
    to_token: if True, set field values to tokens; otherwise to words

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.set_mwt_expansions
documentation:
Extend the multi-word tokens annotated by tokenizer. A list of list of expansions
will be expected for each multi-word token.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.get_mwt_expansions
documentation:
Get the multi-word tokens. For training, return a list of
(multi-word token, extended multi-word token); otherwise, return a list of
multi-word token only.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.build_ents
documentation: Build the list of entities by iterating over all words. Return all entities as a list.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.iter_words
documentation: An iterator that returns all of the words in this Document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.iter_tokens
documentation: An iterator that returns all of the tokens in this Document.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Document.to_dict
documentation: Dumps the whole document into a list of list of dictionary for each token in each sentence in the doc.
A document class that stores attributes of a document and carries a list of sentences.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.entities

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.build_ents
documentation:
Build the list of entities by iterating over all tokens. Return all entities as a list.

Note that unlike other attributes, since NER requires raw text, the actual tagging are always
performed at and attached to the `Token`s, instead of `Word`s.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.build_dependencies
documentation:
Build the dependency graph for this sentence. Each dependency graph entry is
a list of (head, deprel, word).

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.print_dependencies
documentation: Print the dependencies for this sentence.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.dependencies_string
documentation: Dump the dependencies for this sentence into string.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.print_tokens
documentation: Print the tokens for this sentence.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.tokens_string
documentation: Dump the tokens for this sentence into string.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.print_words
documentation: Print the words for this sentence.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.words_string
documentation: Dump the words for this sentence into string.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Sentence.to_dict
documentation: Dumps the sentence into a list of dictionary for each token in the sentence.
A sentence class that stores attributes of a sentence and carries a list of tokens.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.words
documentation: Access the list of syntactic words underlying this token.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.words
documentation: Set this token's list of underlying syntactic words.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.start_char
documentation: Access the start character index for this token in the raw text.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.end_char
documentation: Access the end character index for this token in the raw text.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.ner
documentation: Access the NER tag of this token. Example: 'B-ORG'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.ner
documentation: Set the token's NER tag. Example: 'B-ORG'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.to_dict
documentation:
Dumps the token into a list of dictionary for this token with its extended words
if the token is a multi-word token.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Token.pretty_print
documentation: Print this token with its extended words in one line.

A token class that stores attributes of a token and carries a list of words. A token corresponds to a unit in the raw
text. In some languages such as English, a token has a one-to-one mapping to a word, while in other languages such as French,
a (multi-word) token might be expanded into multiple words that carry syntactic annotations.

documentation: Access the text of this word. Example: 'The'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.text

documentation: Access the treebank-specific part-of-speech of this word. Example: 'NNP'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.xpos
documentation: Set the word's treebank-specific part-of-speech value. Example: 'NNP'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.feats
documentation: Access the morphological features of this word. Example: 'Gender=Fem'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.feats
documentation: Set this word's morphological features. Example: 'Gender=Fem'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.head
documentation: Access the id of the governer of this word.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.head
documentation: Set the word's governor id value.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.deprel
documentation: Access the dependency relation of this word. Example: 'nmod'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.deprel
documentation: Set the word's dependency relation value. Example: 'nmod'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.deps
documentation: Access the dependencies of this word.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.deps
documentation: Set the word's dependencies value.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.misc
documentation: Access the miscellaneousness of this word.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.misc
documentation: Set the word's miscellaneousness value.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.parent
documentation:
Access the parent token of this word. In the case of a multi-word token, a token can be the parent of
multiple words. Note that this should return a reference to the parent token object.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.parent
documentation:
Set this word's parent token. In the case of a multi-word token, a token can be the parent of
multiple words. Note that value here should be a reference to the parent token object.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.pos
documentation: Access the universal part-of-speech of this word. Example: 'NOUN'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.pos
documentation: Set the word's universal part-of-speech value. Example: 'NOUN'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.to_dict
documentation: Dumps the word into a dictionary.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Word.pretty_print
documentation: Print the word in one line.
A word class that stores attributes of a word.

Construct a span given a span entry or a list of tokens. A valid reference to a doc
must be provided to construct a span (otherwise the text of the span cannot be initialized).

documentation: Access the parent doc of this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.doc

documentation: Access the text of this span. Example: 'Stanford University'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.text
documentation: Set the span's text value. Example: 'Stanford University'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.tokens
documentation: Access reference to a list of tokens that correspond to this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.tokens
documentation: Set the span's list of tokens.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.words
documentation: Access reference to a list of words that correspond to this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.words
documentation: Set the span's list of words.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.type
documentation: Access the type of this span. Example: 'PERSON'

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.type
documentation: Set the type of this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.start_char
documentation: Access the start character offset of this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.start_char
documentation: Set the start character offset of this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.end_char
documentation: Access the end character offset of this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.end_char
documentation: Set the end character offset of this span.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.to_dict
documentation: Dumps the span into a dictionary.

methods.name: phonlp.models.common.doc.Span.pretty_print
documentation: Print the span in one line.

A span class that stores attributes of a textual span. A span can be typed.
A range of objects (e.g., entity mentions) can be represented as spans.

modules.name: phonlp

modules.name: phonlp.run_script

modules.name: phonlp.utils.conll
documentation: Utility functions for the loading and conversion of CoNLL-format files.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.load_conll
documentation:
Load the file or string into the CoNLL-U format data.
Input: file or string reader, where the data is in CoNLL-U format.
Output: a list of list of list for each token in each sentence in the data, where the innermost list represents
all fields of a token.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.convert_conll
documentation:
Convert the CoNLL-U format input data to a dictionary format output data.
Input: list of token fields loaded from the CoNLL-U format data, where the outmost list represents a list of sentences, and the inside list represents all fields of a token.
Output: a list of list of dictionaries for each token in each sentence in the document.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.convert_conll_token
documentation:
Convert the CoNLL-U format input token to the dictionary format output token.
Input: a list of all CoNLL-U fields for the token.
Output: a dictionary that maps from field name to value.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.conll2dict
documentation: Load the CoNLL-U format data from file or string into lists of dictionaries.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.convert_dict
documentation:
Convert the dictionary format input data to the CoNLL-U format output data. This is the reverse function of
`convert_conll`.
Input: dictionary format data, which is a list of list of dictionaries for each token in each sentence in the data.
Output: CoNLL-U format data, which is a list of list of list for each token in each sentence in the data.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.convert_token_dict
documentation:
Convert the dictionary format input token to the CoNLL-U format output token. This is the reverse function of
`convert_conll_token`.
Input: dictionary format token, which is a dictionaries for the token.
Output: CoNLL-U format token, which is a list for the token.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.conll_as_string
documentation: Dump the loaded CoNLL-U format list data to string.

methods.name: phonlp.utils.conll.CoNLL.dict2conll
documentation: Convert the dictionary format input data to the CoNLL-U format output data and write to a file.

modules.name: phonlp.utils.conll18_ud_eval
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
Compatible with Python 2.7 and 3.2+, can be used either as a module
or a standalone executable.
Copyright 2017, 2018 Institute of Formal and Applied Linguistics (UFAL),
Faculty of Mathematics and Physics, Charles University, Czech Republic.
This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
Authors: Milan Straka, Martin Popel <surname@ufal.mff.cuni.cz>
Changelog:
- [12 Apr 2018] Version 0.9: Initial release.
- [19 Apr 2018] Version 1.0: Fix bug in MLAS (duplicate entries in functional_children).
Add --counts option.
- [02 May 2018] Version 1.1: When removing spaces to match gold and system characters,
consider all Unicode characters of category Zs instead of
just ASCII space.
- [25 Jun 2018] Version 1.2: Use python3 in the she-bang (instead of python).
In Python2, make the whole computation use `unicode` strings.
Command line usage
conll18_ud_eval.py [-v] gold_conllu_file system_conllu_file
- if no -v is given, only the official CoNLL18 UD Shared Task evaluation metrics
are printed
- if -v is given, more metrics are printed (as precision, recall, F1 score,
and in case the metric is computed on aligned words also accuracy on these):
- Tokens: how well do the gold tokens match system tokens
- Sentences: how well do the gold sentences match system sentences
- Words: how well can the gold words be aligned to system words
- UPOS: using aligned words, how well does UPOS match
- XPOS: using aligned words, how well does XPOS match
- UFeats: using aligned words, how well does universal FEATS match
- AllTags: using aligned words, how well does UPOS+XPOS+FEATS match
- Lemmas: using aligned words, how well does LEMMA match
- UAS: using aligned words, how well does HEAD match
- LAS: using aligned words, how well does HEAD+DEPREL(ignoring subtypes) match
- CLAS: using aligned words with content DEPREL, how well does
HEAD+DEPREL(ignoring subtypes) match
- MLAS: using aligned words with content DEPREL, how well does
HEAD+DEPREL(ignoring subtypes)+UPOS+UFEATS+FunctionalChildren(DEPREL+UPOS+UFEATS) match
- BLEX: using aligned words with content DEPREL, how well does
HEAD+DEPREL(ignoring subtypes)+LEMMAS match
- if -c is given, raw counts of correct/gold_total/system_total/aligned words are printed
instead of precision/recall/F1/AlignedAccuracy for all metrics.
API usage
- load_conllu(file)
- loads CoNLL-U file from given file object to an internal representation
- the file object should return str in both Python 2 and Python 3
- raises UDError exception if the given file cannot be loaded
- evaluate(gold_ud, system_ud)
- evaluate the given gold and system CoNLL-U files (loaded with load_conllu)
- raises UDError if the concatenated tokens of gold and system file do not match
- returns a dictionary with the metrics described above, each metric having
three fields: precision, recall and f1
Description of token matching
In order to match tokens of gold file and system file, we consider the text
resulting from concatenation of gold tokens and text resulting from
concatenation of system tokens. These texts should match -- if they do not,
the evaluation fails.
If the texts do match, every token is represented as a range in this original
text, and tokens are equal only if their range is the same.
Description of word matching
When matching words of gold file and system file, we first match the tokens.
The words which are also tokens are matched as tokens, but words in multi-word
tokens have to be handled differently.
To handle multi-word tokens, we start by finding "multi-word spans".
Multi-word span is a span in the original text such that
- it contains at least one multi-word token
- all multi-word tokens in the span (considering both gold and system ones)
are completely inside the span (i.e., they do not "stick out")
- the multi-word span is as small as possible
For every multi-word span, we align the gold and system words completely
inside this span using LCS on their FORMs. The words not intersecting
(even partially) any multi-word span are then aligned as tokens.
